# CHIME-DSA Holding Tree Merge Decision

## Finding

The active code home is [`/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS). The holding folder [`/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/research-holding/caltech/ovro/dsa110/chime_dsa_codetections`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/research-holding/caltech/ovro/dsa110/chime_dsa_codetections) is not a git repo, is only about 37 MB, and has 44 remaining files.

Do not merge it wholesale. Most remaining content is stale migration state, local editor/agent state, legacy scripts, or data products that belong in the external data authority, not git. The active FLITS repo already records the newer four-host migration model in [`DATA_LOCATIONS.md`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/DATA_LOCATIONS.md), [`machine_inventory.yaml`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/machine_inventory.yaml), and [`docs/infrastructure/MIGRATION_LOG.md`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/docs/infrastructure/MIGRATION_LOG.md).

## Merge Strategy

1. Preserve FLITS as canonical. Do not copy code or old repo state from the holding folder into FLITS.
2. Review and likely promote only the two missing association CSVs from the holding folder:
   - [`frb_cluster_associations.csv`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/research-holding/caltech/ovro/dsa110/chime_dsa_codetections/frb_cluster_associations.csv)
   - [`frb_halo_associations.csv`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/research-holding/caltech/ovro/dsa110/chime_dsa_codetections/frb_halo_associations.csv)

   Reason: [`notebooks/codetections/interveners.ipynb`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/notebooks/codetections/interveners.ipynb) reads those filenames directly, and FLITS has no matching files. Because [` .gitignore`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/.gitignore) ignores `*.csv`, add a narrow exception if they are committed. Best target: [`notebooks/codetections/data/`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/notebooks/codetections/data/) or [`galaxies/foreground/data/legacy/`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/galaxies/foreground/data/legacy/), with notebook paths updated.
3. Do not promote the 36 MB [`dm/DSA_DM_phase*`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/research-holding/caltech/ovro/dsa110/chime_dsa_codetections/dm/) products into git. The FLITS manifest already tracks the presentation sentinel and external data authority; store or verify these under the external `presentations/` or `dm_budget/` data area instead.
4. Do not promote [`h23_resilient_transfer.sh`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/research-holding/caltech/ovro/dsa110/chime_dsa_codetections/h23_resilient_transfer.sh) as runnable code. FLITS docs explicitly treat it as stale legacy reference and point to [`scripts/migration/h23_to_iacobus.sh`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/scripts/migration/h23_to_iacobus.sh).
5. Treat [`CONSOLIDATION_PLAN.md`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/research-holding/caltech/ovro/dsa110/chime_dsa_codetections/CONSOLIDATION_PLAN.md), [`PROJECT_INVENTORY.md`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/research-holding/caltech/ovro/dsa110/chime_dsa_codetections/PROJECT_INVENTORY.md), and [`GIT_RECONCILIATION_REPORT.md`](/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/research-holding/caltech/ovro/dsa110/chime_dsa_codetections/GIT_RECONCILIATION_REPORT.md) as historical references. If kept in FLITS, place them under `docs/migration/archive/` and label them superseded by the June four-host docs.
6. After targeted promotion and external-data verification, move the entire holding folder into the codetection `_quarantine/` area. Move only; do not delete.

## Constraints

The FLITS worktree is currently dirty on branch `verify-claude-review`; preserve those unrelated changes. Any implementation should happen on a fresh branch or worktree and stage only task-scoped files.

Migration inventory gate already reports retired-host coverage OK, and `scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --migration-status pending` printed no pending entries.

## Verification

- Run `python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --migration-status pending` and `python scripts/query_machine_inventory.py --check-retired-coverage` from FLITS after any doc/data-location edits.
- If the CSVs are promoted, run a small notebook-path smoke test that `pd.read_csv(...)` can locate both files from the intended working directory.
- Run `git status --short --branch` before and after to prove unrelated dirty files were not swept in.
- If any files are moved to quarantine, write a restore note with the original path and target path before moving.
